home.tagline: Sculpture, public art, watercolours and scenography since 1963
home.about_text: Sivert Lindblom is a Swedish sculptor and artist who completed his studies at the Royal Academy of Fine Arts in Stockholm in 1963. Early in his career he drew inspiration from Auguste Rodin, before moving towards abstract and concept-based work with installations and architectural elements. He taught at the School of Architecture in the late 1960s, is a member of the Royal Academy of Fine Arts and the National Board of Public Art, and was Professor of Sculpture at the School of Art in Stockholm. He has carried out a large number of public commissions in collaboration with leading architects, and received the Sergel Prize from the Academy in 1995.
home.contact_sub: Questions about works, loans or collaborations
home.press_quote: »Sivert Lindblom's website is a monument in itself — a digital archive that conveys a life's work in all its complexity and beauty with the same striving for clarity and space as the sculptures themselves.«

portfolio.title: Artistic work 1961–2016
portfolio.subtitle: Four decades of artistic work
portfolio.intro: Four categories document Sivert Lindblom's artistic practice: exhibitions, public commissions, watercolours and scenography.
portfolio.desc_exhibitions: Solo and group exhibitions in Sweden and internationally, from Galerie Buren 1963 to Vandalorum 2016.
portfolio.desc_public: Sculptures and installations in public squares, metro stations, embassies and libraries in Sweden and around the world.
portfolio.desc_watercolors: Approx. 200 watercolour works — a 'mental constructivism' project featuring hermetic architectural geometry.
portfolio.desc_scenography: Set designs for Stockholms Stadsteater and choreographies with Margareta Åsberg and others.
portfolio.no_images: No images available at this time.
portfolio.blasieholmstorg_desc: Two green-patinated bronze horses modelled after the originals at the Basilica of San Marco in Venice. Cast by Herman Bergmans Konstgjuteri AB.

watercolors.title: Watercolours 1975–2012
watercolors.description: Approximately 200 sketch-like watercolour works created as a form of 'mental constructivism', depicting a hermetic architectural landscape. The works are shaped by a strictly controlled graphic technique resulting in a freely applied axonometry.
watercolors.book_note: Fifty works were exhibited at the Royal Academy of Fine Arts in Stockholm, 6 October – 4 November 2012. The book Sivert Lindblom Watercolours etc. (Bullfinch Publishing, 2012) documents the collection.
watercolors.short_description: A series of axonometric architectural visions painted with unmarked Winsor & Newton pigments directly from the box. Peter Cornell describes them as "a flow of ingenious constructions" — a hermetic architectural landscape.
watercolors.context_1: One hundred works were shown in their entirety at Kungl. Konstakademien, Stockholm, October–November 2012 (70 running metres of wall).
watercolors.context_2: Catalogue with texts by Peter Cornell, Jan Öqvist and Catharina Gabrielsson.

texts.title: Criticism, essays & interviews
texts.subtitle: Texts
texts.intro: Texts about and by Sivert Lindblom: critical essays, reviews, interviews and his own writings, 1963–2012.

biography.title: Sivert Lindblom
biography.intro: Sivert Lindblom (b. 1931 in Husby-Rekarne, Södermanland) is one of Sweden's most significant sculptors. He studied at the Royal Academy of Fine Arts in Stockholm 1958–1963. Early in his career he drew inspiration from Auguste Rodin, before moving towards abstract and concept-based work with installations and architectural elements. He taught at the School of Architecture in the late 1960s, is a member of the Royal Academy of Fine Arts and the National Board of Public Art, and was Professor of Sculpture at the School of Art in Stockholm. He has carried out a large number of public commissions in collaboration with leading architects, and received the Sergel Prize from the Academy in 1995.
biography.timeline_4: Royal Academy of Fine Arts, Stockholm
biography.timeline_7: Teacher of form theory, KTH School of Architecture

references.title: Reference material
references.subtitle: Sculpture & Graphics
references.intro: Sculpture series, graphics, film and photographs documenting Sivert Lindblom's artistic production.
references.no_images: No images available at this time.
references.fotografier_intro: This gallery contains images that in one way or another have touched and inspired Sivert Lindblom in his work. His works, however apparently ahistorical, emanate from a long tradition of symbols that artists throughout all ages and cultures have sought in order to manifest the presence of humanity.
references.publicerat_intro: Catalogues, journal articles and books with texts relating to Sivert's artistic practice.
